description_long: This lantern features a tall, post-mounted design with a hexagonal base and a central vertical pole supporting two lantern heads. The main lantern head is larger, with a conical roof and a cylindrical glass enclosure, while a smaller, matching lantern is attached to the side via a horizontal arm. Both lantern heads have faceted, metallic roofs and bases, and the glass enclosures are segmented, likely to diffuse light evenly. The structure is predominantly metallic with a gold finish, giving it a decorative and classic appearance suitable for urban parks or sidewalks. The overall design is symmetrical and functional, with clean lines and a robust build.
description: A gold, post-mounted lantern with two conical-roofed heads—one large and one smaller—featuring cylindrical glass enclosures and a decorative, symmetrical design.
description_view_0: Front view showing both lantern heads aligned vertically, highlighting the symmetry and the faceted roofs.
description_view_1: Angled view revealing the side-mounted smaller lantern and the connecting horizontal arm.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the main lantern head and the central post, with the smaller lantern less visible.
description_view_3: Side view clearly displaying the offset smaller lantern and the supporting arm extending from the main post.
